thread.title: Meghalaya to Implement Strict SOPs on Sirens and Tinted Glasses to Curb VIP Culture | Shillong News - Times of India
thread.title_full: Meghalaya to Implement Strict SOPs on Sirens and Tinted Glasses to Curb VIP Culture | Shillong News - Times of India

author: Times Of India
title: Meghalaya to Implement Strict SOPs on Sirens and Tinted Glasses to Curb VIP Culture | Shillong News - Times of India
text: Shillong: Chief minister Conrad K Sangma Friday announced that the govt will soon come up with an SOP on the use of sirens, tinted glasses and flickering lights in the state and the same will be strictly implemented to minimise the inconvenience caused to the public.“I have been receiving several complaints from citizens about what people are calling VIP culture. It is, of course, a matter of concern. But we also have to look at it from multiple angles. There is a concern about security and safety, which should not be mixed with the aspect of culture. Safety and security are one aspect, which is something we personally feel is important and should not be compromised. But if it becomes more of an attitude and is used in the wrong way, then that is a problem,” the CM said. Conrad said he had a meeting with deputy CM in-charge home (police), Prestone Tynsong, chief secretary and DGP last week. The police dept was asked to prepare an SOP as to who can and who cannot use sirens, tinted glasses and flickering lights. Once it is ready, the SOP will be strictly implemented, including the protocols to be followed while using sirens, flickering lights and other devices, he added.The CM said the SOP will be brought before the cabinet by next week. “There are protocols that need to be followed for those who come under the different categories of security like Z-plus, Z, and Y, and these are defined protocols, which cannot be compromised,” he said.We also published the following articles recentlyMeghalaya govt to come up with SOP on use of sirens, tinted glasses & flickering lightsMeghalaya Chief Minister Conrad K. Sangma addressed public concerns regarding \"VIP culture,\" stating that the government will soon implement a strict SOP for siren, tinted glass, and flickering light usage. Aimed at minimizing public inconvenience while prioritizing security, the SOP will outline protocols for various security categories, ensuring responsible use without compromising safety.115571587UN rights office 'gravely concerned' by use of security law in Hong KongThe UN Human Rights office expressed deep worry over the imprisonment of 45 pro-democracy activists in Hong Kong under the security law. A spokesperson for the office, Jeremy Laurence, conveyed their concerns but affirmed their commitment to maintaining dialogue with Chinese authorities despite the situation.115450873Maharashtra goes to polls today: Battle hinges on sops, jobs, quota & cropMaharashtra's upcoming assembly election is shaping up to be a battleground fueled by cash, caste politics, and farmer concerns. The ruling Mahayuti coalition is banking on welfare schemes and divisive rhetoric, while the opposition MVA is highlighting economic anxieties and regional pride. With both major regional parties fractured, the outcome remains uncertain, potentially hinging on rebel candidates and smaller parties.115467156
